value_picks.name: Cognizant Technology Solutions

pe_discount.detail: Forward PE 8.59 vs implied PE 12.99 ($80 median target / $6.16 fwd EPS) = 33.9% discount.

drawdown.detail: 39.22% below 52w high of $87.03; trading at $52.90, near 52w low of $52.34.

balance_sheet.detail: Net cash $425M (cash $1.517B - debt $1.092B). Current ratio 2.226. D/E reported 7.25 is misleadingly small relative to equity given $14.9B book value.

dividend.detail: Yield 2.5% with payout ratio 27.3%, well covered by FCF.

institutional.detail: Institutional ownership 105.7% of float (heavy inst plus short overlap); 26 analysts, recommendation_mean 2.17 (<2.5).

ai_exposure.detail: Cognizant explicitly markets AI consulting and integration services; secular trend favors IT services firms helping enterprises deploy AI, though long-term threat exists if AI compresses billable-hour services pricing.

deal_radar.assessment: Short interest 9.83% (up 14.6% MoM, 6.56 days to cover) signals bearish bet but creates squeeze potential at this level.

catalysts_general: Trading at 33.9% discount to consensus implied PE based on $80 median target
Forward PE of 8.59 with PEG 0.90 indicates valuation cheap relative to growth
Dividend yield 2.5% with very low 27.3% payout ratio
Net cash balance sheet ($425M) provides downside cushion
Stock at 52w low $52.34 — major technical support if held

downside_scenarios.scenario: Mild PE compression to 8x fwd EPS on macro fear
Recession + IT services budget cuts; revenue growth flattens to 0%
AI disrupts services pricing; margins contract 200bps

ideal_entry.reasoning: At/near 52w low $52.34 with 7.6% FCF yield, 2.5% dividend, and net cash. Limit at $52.50 captures the bottom of recent range.

comparables.note: Larger peer trading at higher multiple despite similar drawdown
Direct peer, faster growth, similar valuation
Indian IT services peer trading at significant premium

value_picks.analyst_take: Best risk/reward of the cohort: only ticker scoring 6/6, trading at 52w low with positive FCF yield, dividend, and net cash. The 33.9% discount to analyst consensus combined with 9.83% short interest (and rising) creates asymmetric upside if Q1 earnings stabilize the IT services budget narrative.

value_picks.name: EPAM Systems, Inc.

pe_discount.detail: Forward PE 8.18 vs implied PE 13.87 ($193 median / $13.92 fwd EPS) = 41.0% discount.

drawdown.detail: 48.87% below 52w high of $222.53; trading $4 above 52w low.

balance_sheet.detail: Net cash $1.152B (cash $1.296B - debt $144M). Current ratio 2.594. Net cash equals 19% of market cap.

institutional.detail: Institutional ownership 113.96% (heavy inst + shorts); 17 analysts, recommendation_mean 1.79 (Buy).

ai_exposure.detail: Pure-play digital engineering and software services; explicitly positioned in AI/data engineering. Loss of Russia/Belarus delivery centers in 2022 already absorbed; current setup is AI-led demand recovery story.

deal_radar.assessment: Short interest 18.35% is very elevated — high-beta (1.73) name with classic squeeze setup if Q1 results stabilize.

catalysts_general: 41% discount to analyst consensus implied PE
Net cash equals 19% of market cap — extreme balance sheet strength
FCF yield 12.08% — highest in the cohort
Short interest 18.35% sets up squeeze on positive surprise
Revenue growth 12.8% — fastest in IT services peers

downside_scenarios.scenario: Tag and break of 52w low on cohort weakness
Bookings deterioration in 1H, growth re-rated to 5%
Severe IT services recession; net cash floor

ideal_entry.reasoning: Near 52w low $109.70 with 12% FCF yield and net cash > 1/5 of market cap. Strong technical floor; limit at $110 captures the gap-fill zone.

comparables.note: Slower-growing peer at slight premium
Mega-cap peer; richer multiple
Direct LatAm-focused peer at higher multiple

value_picks.analyst_take: Highest FCF yield in cohort (12.1%) with biggest analyst upside (target $193, +70%). Net cash 19% of MC means valuation has built-in floor. Only knock is no dividend; otherwise the cleanest risk/reward setup with squeeze optionality from 18% short interest.

value_picks.name: Atlassian Corporation

pe_discount.detail: Forward PE 12.38 vs implied PE 21.66 ($120 median / $5.54 fwd EPS) = 42.8% discount.

drawdown.detail: 70.48% below 52w high of $232.36 — most severe drawdown in cohort.

balance_sheet.detail: Net cash technically positive ($351M = $1.566B cash - $1.215B debt), but current ratio 0.891 (<1.0) and D/E 76 indicates working capital tightness; passes net-cash test but fails liquidity test.

institutional.detail: Institutional ownership 95.35%; 31 analysts, recommendation_mean 1.58 (strongest buy rating in cohort).

ai_exposure.detail: Atlassian Intelligence (AI features in Jira/Confluence) is a monetization vector; collaboration tools benefit from AI-augmented workflows. Threat from Notion/Microsoft Copilot bundling is real but Jira's enterprise lock-in is sticky.

deal_radar.assessment: Short interest 7.92% (rising 28% MoM) shows emerging skepticism. GAAP operating loss (-3.0% margins) despite strong FCF reflects heavy stock-based comp.

catalysts_general: 70% drawdown — most extreme in cohort
23.3% revenue growth — fastest in cohort
84% gross margins — highest in cohort
FCF yield 8.4% despite GAAP loss (heavy SBC)
Trades at 12.4x forward EPS vs SaaS comps at 25-40x

downside_scenarios.scenario: Test of 52w low $56.01
Cloud migration slowdown; CR<1 forces capital action
Enterprise software recession; SBC dilution thesis dominates

ideal_entry.reasoning: Below 50d SMA $71 and closer to 52w low $56.01; current ratio <1 demands a margin of safety. $60 entry is mid-point between current and 52w low.

comparables.note: Cheaper enterprise SaaS peer with less drawdown
Profitable software comp at higher multiple
Direct collaboration competitor at growth premium

value_picks.analyst_take: Best optical setup (70% drawdown, 23% growth, strongest analyst rating) but balance sheet liquidity is the watch-item: current ratio 0.891 means working capital is tight. Strong analyst conviction (rec_mean 1.58, lowest in cohort) but price needs to confirm — wait for break above 50d SMA $71 or revisit of 52w low for better entry.

value_picks.name: Accenture plc

pe_discount.detail: Forward PE 11.99 vs implied PE 16.77 ($250 median / $14.91 fwd EPS) = 28.5% discount (15-30% borderline).

balance_sheet.detail: Net cash $1.06B (cash $9.41B - debt $8.35B). Current ratio 1.336. D/E 25.5 — investment grade.

dividend.detail: Yield 3.65% with payout 51.0% — sustainable, covered by FCF.

ai_exposure.detail: Accenture is the largest IT services firm and explicitly positioned as the implementation partner for enterprise AI; Generative AI bookings already a stated growth driver. Same long-term services-pricing risk as CTSH but scale advantage.

deal_radar.assessment: Short interest only 3.27% but rising 22.5% MoM — bears building. ROE 24.8% remains highest-quality in cohort.

catalysts_general: FCF yield 11.06% on a mega-cap — rare combination
Dividend yield 3.65% with FCF coverage 2x
ROE 24.8% — highest quality metric in cohort
45% drawdown on a $110B blue-chip is unusual
Trading at 12x forward EPS vs 5y average ~25x

downside_scenarios.scenario: Break of 52w low on consulting downgrade
Federal contract risk + Europe slowdown
AI-driven services pricing reset; revenue flat 2 years

ideal_entry.reasoning: At 52w low $173.65 — quintessential mean-reversion bid for highest-quality IT services franchise. Limit $174 lifts above 52w low minimum.

comparables.note: Cheaper smaller peer
Faster-growing pure-play engineering peer
Mega-cap peer at premium

value_picks.analyst_take: Quality at distressed price: ROE 24.8% and 11% FCF yield on a $110B name is rare. The 28.5% discount to consensus is borderline (not pass) but the dividend and balance sheet quality earn the BUY. Federal contract overhang is the explanation for drawdown; sized small to allow averaging into low $170s if it breaks.

value_picks.name: Workday, Inc.

pe_discount.detail: Forward PE 9.84 vs implied PE 14.27 ($177.5 median / $12.44 fwd EPS) = 31.0% discount (just above 30%).

balance_sheet.detail: Net cash $1.622B (cash $5.443B - debt $3.821B). Current ratio 1.322. D/E 49.0 — passes net-cash test.

ai_exposure.detail: Workday is layering AI agents into HR/Finance workflows; Illuminate platform announced as AI-native. Risk is that AI commoditizes some HCM/FP&A modules but Workday's data moat in employee records is meaningful.

deal_radar.assessment: Short interest 9.23% (up 24% MoM) reflects skepticism around enterprise IT spend. Forward PE 9.84 is unusual for a 14.5%-growth SaaS name.

catalysts_general: Forward PE 9.84 on a 14.5% revenue grower is anomalous
Earnings growth 62.9% reflects margin inflection
PEG 0.47 — most attractive in cohort
Net cash $1.6B = 5% of market cap
55% drawdown for a SaaS leader on no fundamental break

downside_scenarios.scenario: Test of 52w low $110.36
Enterprise IT spending cycle deepens; bookings flat
AI displaces HCM functions; revenue growth halves

ideal_entry.reasoning: Just above 52w low $110.36; price has hugged 20d SMA $122.34 — needs confirmed bounce or revisit of low for asymmetric setup.

comparables.note: Faster-growing SaaS at higher multiple but worse balance sheet
Profitable SaaS leader at premium
Larger SaaS comp at significant premium

value_picks.analyst_take: Numerically attractive (PEG 0.47, 9.6% FCF yield, 14.5% growth) but the lack of a dividend and very recent rollover means waiting for a tag of $110-115 zone gives better odds. WATCHLIST until either 52w low retest or break above 50d SMA $130.

value_picks.name: Intuit Inc.

pe_discount.detail: Forward PE 14.67 vs implied PE 22.28 ($590 median / $26.48 fwd EPS) = 34.2% discount.

balance_sheet.detail: Net debt $3.91B (cash $2.975B - debt $6.888B), but D/E 36.1 (<50) AND current ratio 1.323 (>1.2) satisfies the borderline-pass test.

dividend.detail: Yield 1.19% (<2%) with payout 29.2% (<90%); falls into borderline bucket.

institutional.detail: Institutional ownership 90.94%; 32 analysts, recommendation_mean 1.47 (strongest in cohort).

ai_exposure.detail: Intuit Assist is Intuit's AI counter-strategy, but TurboTax/QuickBooks face direct disintermediation risk from generative AI tax-prep and bookkeeping startups. Enterprise moat (Credit Karma, payments) helps but core consumer franchise is the most AI-vulnerable in this cohort.

deal_radar.assessment: Short interest only 3.9% but up 30.1% MoM — bears building on AI-disruption thesis. ROE 23.5% and 80.9% gross margins remain best-in-class.

catalysts_general: 52% drawdown — first major reset in 5 years
Forward PE 14.67 vs 5y average 30+
Earnings growth 48.5%, profit margins 21.6% — best in cohort
Analyst rec_mean 1.47 — strongest conviction in cohort
Trades at 34% discount to $590 consensus median target

downside_scenarios.scenario: Test of 52w low $342.11
AI-disruption thesis hits Q4 tax season guidance
QuickBooks share loss to AI bookkeeping startups confirmed

ideal_entry.reasoning: Near 52w low $342.11. AI-disruption overhang requires a margin of safety; $350 limit captures the test of the low without chasing.

comparables.note: Cheaper enterprise SaaS peer with similar drawdown
Higher growth, lower quality balance sheet
Software peer with similar AI-threat narrative

value_picks.analyst_take: Quality franchise at first major sale in years, but AI threat to TurboTax/QuickBooks core is the real reason it trades here — and it's not resolved. Score 5.0 plus highest analyst conviction (rec_mean 1.47) argues for owning, but FCF yield <5% and 4% net debt mean entry should be at/below 52w low. Watchlist until the AI disruption narrative either confirms or breaks.

growth_picks.name: Applovin Corporation

revenue_growth.detail: Revenue growth of 65.9% — well above 30% threshold.

gross_margins.detail: Gross margins 87.9% — exceptional, well above 65% bar.

earnings_accel.detail: Earnings growth 84.7% vs revenue growth 65.9% — clear operating leverage.

capital_efficiency.detail: ROE of 212.9% — extreme capital efficiency, partly due to thin book value ($6.31).

short_dynamics.detail: SI 4.26% (<5%) and falling -5.03% MoM — squeeze pressure absent, sentiment improving.

ai_exposure.detail: Axon AI engine is the core moat — drives ad-targeting performance and fuels the company's revenue/margin profile. AI is not just a tailwind, it is the business.

deal_radar.assessment: No options or capex data provided. Down 40% from 52w high yet trading 1.6% above SMA20 and 1.8% above SMA50 — short-term momentum reasserting after a meaningful drawdown.

catalysts_general: Trading 30.2% below analyst mean target of $639.36 (43.2% upside).
26 of 30 analysts rate strong-buy/buy; recommendation_mean 1.47.
Forward P/E 21.9 against 65.9% revenue growth — PEG 1.25 is reasonable for the growth profile.
Down 40.1% from 52w high creates a structurally cheaper entry than late-2024.
FCF of $2.7B against $150B mcap — ~1.8% FCF yield, modest but real.
Beta 2.5 — high reward, high risk in regime moves.

downside_scenarios.scenario: Tech multiple compression — retest SMA50 zone, ~10% downside.
Analyst low-target case — broader ad-tech rerate.
Retest 52w low — would imply growth deceleration or AI ad-tech narrative break.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Below SMA20/SMA50 cluster (~$438) but above the recent recovery base. Buys at 20.2x forward against 66% revenue growth and 87% gross margins.

comparables.note: Pure-play ad-tech peer; lower margin profile than APP.
Reference point for AI-driven ad-tech multiple at scale.

growth_picks.analyst_take: AppLovin is the rare 6/6 framework name — exceptional growth, best-in-class margins, real operating leverage, and improving short positioning. The 40% drawdown from highs creates a credible BUY at ~22x forward earnings against 66% revenue growth, though the 2.5 beta means sizing matters.

growth_picks.name: Newmont Corporation

revenue_growth.detail: Revenue growth 45.8% — clear pass against 30% bar; gold-cycle leverage.

gross_margins.detail: Gross margins 67.4% — just over the 65% threshold.

earnings_accel.detail: Earnings growth 78.6% vs revenue 45.8% — strong operating leverage off cost discipline + gold price.

capital_efficiency.detail: ROE 25.8% — above 20% bar, materially better than typical gold-major levels.

short_dynamics.detail: SI 2.03% and falling -10.55% MoM — sentiment improving, no squeeze tail risk.

ai_exposure.detail: Gold mining is largely indifferent to AI demand. AI may marginally improve ore-body modelling and operations, but it is not a thesis driver.

deal_radar.assessment: No options or capex data provided. Down 17.6% from 52w high; price below both SMA20 and SMA50 — short-term weakness within an intact uptrend (52w low $48.27 vs current $111).

catalysts_general: Trading 22.8% below analyst mean target of $143.96 (29.6% upside).
17 of 21 analysts rate strong-buy/buy; recommendation_mean 1.67.
Forward P/E only 9.8 against 45.8% revenue growth — exceptionally cheap for the growth profile.
FCF of $9.8B against $118B mcap — ~8.3% FCF yield, very high for a major.
Net debt of -$3.2B (cash > debt) post-portfolio sales.
Dividend yield 0.94% with low 13.1% payout ratio leaves room for buybacks/hikes.
Beta 0.475 — defensive characteristics layered on top of cyclical leverage.

downside_scenarios.scenario: Gold pullback to $2,800-2,900/oz; -10% from current.
Risk-on rotation, gold thesis loses urgency.
Analyst low-target case — significant gold price reset.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Below current and SMA50 with room to scale lower. At 9.3x forward against 78.6% earnings growth, multiple is structurally cheap; FCF yield approaches 9% at this level.

comparables.note: Higher-multiple gold peer; quality premium.
Barrick — closer scale comp; NEM cheaper.

growth_picks.analyst_take: Newmont scores a clean 6/6 with the most counter-intuitive multiple in the cohort: a forward P/E under 10 on 46% revenue growth and 79% earnings growth. The setup is a defensive beta with cyclical operating leverage, real FCF, and improving short positioning — a high-conviction BUY at the analyst-low cluster.

growth_picks.name: Eli Lilly and Company

revenue_growth.detail: Revenue growth 42.6% — well above 30%, GLP-1 driven.

earnings_accel.detail: Earnings growth 51.4% vs revenue 42.6% — operating leverage as Mounjaro/Zepbound scale.

capital_efficiency.detail: ROE 101.2% — extreme due to buyback-driven equity base reduction; well above 20%.

short_dynamics.detail: SI is very low at 1.02% (<5%) but rising +18.06% MoM. Absolute level is benign; rate-of-change suggests modest positioning shift.

ai_exposure.detail: AI accelerates drug discovery, target identification, and trial design. Lilly's scale and data assets make it a prime beneficiary, though near-term thesis remains GLP-1, not AI.

deal_radar.assessment: No options or capex data provided. Down 17.6% from 52w high after a -8.7% 50d return; trading just above SMA20 but below SMA50 — chop within a longer uptrend off the $623 low.

catalysts_general: Trading 22.3% below analyst mean target of $1,202 (28.7% upside).
23 of 30 analysts rate strong-buy/buy; recommendation_mean 1.71.
Forward P/E 21.9 — below market multiple for a 42.6% revenue grower.
Free cash flow only $1.95B (manufacturing capex absorbing operating cash); not a yield story.
Down 17.6% from 52w high creates a measured re-entry zone for a name that has been hard to enter on weakness.
Low beta (0.50) — provides ballast in a high-beta cohort.

downside_scenarios.scenario: Analyst low-target case — GLP-1 competitive erosion fears intensify.
Multi-quarter slowdown narrative; broader pharma rerate.
Retest 52w low — would imply major thesis break (oral GLP-1 disappointment, generic pressure).

ideal_entry.reasoning: Approximately midway between current and analyst-low support; ~20.7x forward on 51% earnings growth is durable. Allows for further drawdown without breaching the 52w-low support zone.

comparables.note: Direct GLP-1 peer; slower but cheaper.
Reference for slower-growing large-cap pharma multiple floor.

growth_picks.analyst_take: Lilly scores 5.5/6 on the back of GLP-1 dominance, with the lone borderline being a low-but-rising short interest that is too small to matter. The 17% drawdown plus a forward P/E of 22 against 51% earnings growth is one of the better quality-growth setups in megacap pharma — BUY with measured sizing.

growth_picks.name: Teradyne, Inc.

revenue_growth.detail: Revenue growth 87.0% — cyclical recovery in semi-test booming.

gross_margins.detail: Gross margins 58.7% — within 50-65% borderline band, semi-equipment-typical.

earnings_accel.detail: Earnings growth 314.8% vs revenue 87.0% — explosive operating leverage off the prior cyclical trough.

short_dynamics.detail: SI 3.62% (<5%) and falling -5.32% MoM — sentiment confirming the rerate.

ai_exposure.detail: AI chip volume and complexity (HBM, advanced packaging, SLT) drive incremental test intensity. TER is a direct beneficiary of the AI silicon buildout cycle.

deal_radar.assessment: No options or capex data provided. +42.5% over 90d — momentum extreme. Trading below SMA20 ($363.71) but above SMA50 ($329.21) — short-term consolidation after a sharp rally.

catalysts_general: Trading 3.4% below analyst mean target of $355.53 (3.5% upside) — analysts have already caught up.
11 of 18 analysts rate buy; recommendation_mean 1.89 — most cautious of the cohort.
Forward P/E 36.3 — full multiple even after accounting for the cyclical earnings lift.
Earnings growth of 314.8% off a low base — flatters the framework but is fundamentally cyclical, not secular.
Up 470% from 52w low — significant late-cycle entry risk.
FCF $292M against $54B mcap — only ~0.5% yield, scant cushion.

downside_scenarios.scenario: Mild semi-cycle pullback; retest 50d level zone.
Analyst low-target case — semi-test growth slows.
Cycle break — capex pause from large foundries.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Materially below SMA50 and provides margin of safety on a cyclical name where 314% earnings growth normalizes lower. ~30x forward on a fully cycle-rerate name is the upper bound of acceptable.

comparables.note: Process-control semi-equipment peer; cheaper, lower growth.
Front-end semi-equipment; structural multiple floor.

growth_picks.analyst_take: Teradyne's framework looks strong, but the numbers flatter a cyclical recovery off a deep trough — 87% revenue growth is unlikely to repeat, and 36x forward earnings already discounts the AI test thesis. WATCHLIST: a meaningful pullback to ~$280 would unlock a high-quality cyclical entry; chasing here invites timing risk.

growth_picks.name: Interactive Brokers Group, Inc.

gross_margins.detail: Gross margins 93.0% — exceptional, electronic broker scale economics.

rule_of_40.detail: 16.8 + 76.8 = 93.6 — far above 50; margin profile carries the score.

earnings_accel.detail: Earnings growth 21.6% vs revenue 16.8% — modest but real operating leverage.

short_dynamics.detail: SI 2.71% (<5%) and falling -14.08% MoM — strong sentiment improvement.

ai_exposure.detail: AI marginally helps client tools and risk management, but the brokerage thesis is rates, account growth, and DARTs — AI is not a meaningful tailwind or threat.

deal_radar.assessment: No options or capex data provided. Only 4.1% below 52w high, +17.4% over 20d — strong relative momentum, name acting as a financials leader.

catalysts_general: Trading 7.5% below analyst mean target of $85.90 (8.1% upside) — limited cushion.
8 of 10 analysts rate strong-buy/buy; recommendation_mean 1.70.
Forward P/E 27.9 — reasonable for 22% earnings growth and 93% gross margins.
Negative enterprise value -$41.5B (massive cash position $124.6B reflects client custody, not free cash) — an artifact of the brokerage model.
Net interest income beneficiary of higher-for-longer rate regime.
Beta 1.26 — moderate market sensitivity.

downside_scenarios.scenario: Rate cuts compress NII; financials sector pullback.
Analyst low-target case — DART growth disappoints.
Aggressive rate cuts plus market drawdown — re-rates lower.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Modest pullback toward SMA20 zone; ~25.6x forward gives a reasonable entry on a 22% earnings grower with stickiest of revenues.

comparables.note: Slower-growing brokerage peer; rate-sensitivity peer.
Diversified investment bank; reference for financials multiple floor.

growth_picks.analyst_take: IBKR is the cohort's quality-financials pick: 93% gross margins, 23% ROE, falling short interest, and a price within 4% of all-time highs. The borderline revenue growth is the only blemish; at 28x forward this is a BUY for relative-strength compounders with margin of safety on a modest pullback.

growth_picks.name: EQT Corporation

gross_margins.detail: Gross margins 81.0% — exceptional for E&P, low-cost basin position.

earnings_accel.detail: Earnings growth 490.0% vs revenue 49.9% — extreme operating leverage off depressed prior-period comp.

capital_efficiency.detail: ROE 13.4% — within 10-20% borderline band, capital-intensive E&P typical.

short_dynamics.detail: SI 3.43% (<5%) and falling -24.11% MoM — strongest short-decline in the cohort.

ai_exposure.detail: AI data-center power demand is bullish for natural gas as the marginal grid fuel. EQT is the largest US gas producer and a direct beneficiary of structural demand shift.

deal_radar.assessment: No options or capex data provided. Trading 12% below 52w high, just above SMA20 and below SMA50 — consolidation pattern, no strong momentum either way.

catalysts_general: Trading 14.6% below analyst mean target of $70.36 (17.1% upside).
19 of 25 analysts rate strong-buy/buy; recommendation_mean 1.60.
Forward P/E 12.9 — cheap against the AI/gas demand thesis.
FCF of $2.5B against $37.6B mcap — ~6.7% FCF yield.
Earnings growth 490% off the 2024 trough — base-effect, not durable run-rate.
Beta 0.69 — defensive ballast vs. cyclical commodity exposure.
Dividend yield 1.10% with low 12.2% payout ratio.

downside_scenarios.scenario: Analyst low-target case — winter heating demand softens.
Natural gas $2.50/mmbtu environment; AI-data-center demand slower than expected.
Retest 52w low — would imply gas price reset and AI thesis pause.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Just below analyst-low target zone and meaningfully under SMA50. ~12x forward on the largest US gas producer with the AI-power-demand tailwind is structurally attractive.

comparables.note: Antero — Marcellus peer; similar gas-leverage thesis.
Range Resources — Appalachian peer; reference for basin valuation.

growth_picks.analyst_take: EQT is the cohort's contrarian-energy compounder: largest US gas producer, 6.7% FCF yield, falling short interest, and AI data-center power demand as a multi-year tailwind. The 13.4% ROE is the only blemish on what is otherwise a 12.9x forward earnings BUY with a measurable margin of safety to the 52w low.
